PR Title: Add user authentication middleware

Description: Implements JWT-based authentication middleware. Validates tokens on protected routes, extracts user context, handles expired tokens gracefully. Adds rate limiting per-user.

Diff summary: 3 files changed — middleware/auth.py (new, 120 lines), routes/protected.py (modified, +15 lines), tests/test_auth.py (new, 85 lines)

PR Title: Refactor database connection pooling

Description: Migrates from per-request connections to a connection pool using asyncpg. Adds health checks, configurable pool size, and graceful shutdown. Expected to reduce p99 latency by 40%.

Diff summary: 5 files changed — db/pool.py (new), db/connection.py (rewritten), config.py (+10 lines), tests/test_pool.py (new), docker-compose.yml (+3 lines)

PR Title: Implement webhook retry logic

Description: Adds exponential backoff retry for failed webhook deliveries. Max 5 retries over 24 hours. Failed webhooks are logged to dead letter queue. Adds monitoring metrics.

Diff summary: 4 files changed — webhooks/delivery.py (+80 lines), webhooks/retry.py (new, 60 lines), monitoring/metrics.py (+5 lines), tests/test_webhooks.py (+45 lines)

PR Title: Migrate billing system to event sourcing

Description: This is a major architectural change to the billing system. Instead of mutable subscription records, we now store billing events (created, upgraded, downgraded, cancelled, charged, refunded) and derive current state. This enables full audit trail, temporal queries, and simpler proration logic.

Key changes:
- New event store (PostgreSQL + JSONB)
- Event projections for current subscription state
- Migration script for existing 50K subscriptions
- Backward-compatible API (existing endpoints work unchanged)
- New event replay endpoint for debugging

Risk: The migration script runs on production data. Tested on staging (10K records) successfully.

Diff summary: 22 files changed, +1,800 lines, -600 lines. Core: billing/events.py, billing/projections.py, billing/migration.py. Tests: 12 new test files.

Instead of mutable subscription records, we now store billing events (created, upgraded, downgraded, cancelled, charged, refunded) and derive current state. This enables full audit trail, temporal queries, and simpler proration logic.

Key changes:
- New event store (PostgreSQL + JSONB)
- Event projections for current subscription state
- Migration script for existing 50K subscriptions
- Backward-compatible API (existing endpoints work unchanged)
- New event replay endpoint for debugging

Risk: The migration script runs on production data. Tested on staging (10K records) successfully.

Diff summary: 22 files changed, +1,800 lines, -600 lines. Core: billing/events.py, billing/projections.py, billing/migration.py. Tests: 12 new test files.
